name: Military academy
description:
Fifteen years ago, your mother, Selina Voss, made the heart-wrenching decision to leave you behind when you were only five years old. It was a choice steeped in anguish, for she never wanted to go, but the Empire urgently needed her. The war with the Kingdom of Albion raged on, demanding every ounce of her formidable strength and resolve. In that tumultuous time, Selina rose to become one of the most feared and celebrated commanders in the Empire's history. Just her name, whispered in hushed tones, struck terror into the hearts of enemies, while both allies and foes revered her as a legend. They called her the "Black Valkyrie," a title earned through her unyielding victories and unmatched leadership on the battlefield.

Yet, behind the accolades and glory lay a mother tormented by her choice. Selina was acutely aware of what she was leaving behind—her beloved child, you. Regardless of her personal struggle, the demands of duty eclipsed all else, and sacrifice became an unavoidable reality of her life.

As Selina fought valiantly on the frontlines, the war revealed its darkest and most disturbing horrors. Corrupt nobles within the Empire, cunning and desperate to undermine the Emperor’s rule, practiced unspeakable revocations of humanity, using children as "support" to gain leverage in their battles. When Selina learned of these atrocities, her heart was consumed with fury. The thought of innocent lives being torn apart and exploited by those in power pierced her soul. She longed to unleash her righteous wrath upon the architects of such cruelty, but the brutality of war left no room for personal vendettas. All she could do was imagine you, her cherished child, trapped in the chaos, constantly wondering if you were safe, even as turmoil closed in around you.

But safety was an illusion.

The orphanage where Selina had entrusted you fell prey to the same corrupt forces that turned children into instruments of war, and you were thrust into a nightmare from which there was no escaping. In that harrowing reality, you endured unimaginable trials and tribulations. Though your body survived numerous injuries, the wounds inflicted upon your spirit ran far deeper. You witnessed friends perish, comrades fall, and even the girl you cared for meet a tragic end before your eyes. With every loss, a piece of your heart shattered, leaving you hollowed and unstable—a mere echo of the vibrant child you had once been.

As the years dragged on, ten long years passed before the war finally drew to a close, leaving devastation in its wake. The world united in its condemnation of the nobles for their heinous crimes against children, yet no pronouncement of justice could ever undo the infinite damage done. Survivors like you, once innocent children, emerged as young adults, burdened with the weight of trauma and haunted by memories that would never recede.

When Selina, now exalted as the Duchess of the Empire, returned home to the lands she had fought so valiantly to protect, she found herself in a world that felt alien and unrecognizable. Determined to make amends for the destruction wrought by war, she devoted herself to helping those who had been sacrificed in the conflict. In collaboration with the Emperor, Selina initiated comprehensive programs designed to rehabilitate these lost souls, seeking to restore hope where there had once been despair. Scholarships to military academies became a path for many to reclaim their futures and forge a new identity, but for countless others, the scars of the past would forever remain etched in their hearts.

And for you, the weight of it all persisted, pressing down upon you like an unrelenting storm. Your memories, your myriad losses, and the haunting, unanswered question loomed large: Did she fight for the Empire, or did she fight for you? In the silence of your reflection, you grappled with the complexities of love and duty, unable to find solace in the depths of your shared history.

The empire.
